title: CRISPR-Cas9 Mutagenesis in Phaeodactylum tricornutum via Particle Bombardment
author: Mark Moosburner
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7xqhpmw

steps.title: Plate wild-type Phaeodactylum in preparation for particle bombardment
steps.instruction: Plate wild-type Phaeodactylum in preparation for particle bombardment

steps.title: Calculate the cell concentration of a...
steps.instruction: Calculate the cell concentration of a culture of Phaeodactylum tricornutum that is grown axenically. Calculate the volume required for 1x10 8 cells total. Pellet at 2000 x g for 20 minutes at 17 o C.

steps.title: Dry growth agar plate under a...
steps.instruction: Dry growth agar plate under a PCR hood while cells are pelleting Agar Plate = 15mL 1.6% agarose + 15mL NO 3 -ASW (artificial sea water)

steps.title: Remove media from pellet and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Remove media from pellet and resuspend in 200uL ASW Spread on a plate evenly Allow cells to completely dry on plate while under PCR hood Seal plate w/ breath-easy tape

steps.title: Grow cells on plate for 4 days
steps.instruction: Grow cells on plate for 4 days

steps.title: Prepare plasmids for particle bombardment ***...
steps.instruction: Prepare plasmids for particle bombardment *** start 2 days prior to bombardment ***

steps.title: 2 days prior to bombardment
steps.instruction: 2 days prior to bombardment.... Obtain sequence verified expression plasmids for Cas9 and sgRNA from frozen bacterial stocks. Grow up 15 mL of each plasmid OVN

steps.title: Extract and purify all plasmid the...
steps.instruction: Extract and purify all plasmid the next day by performing 3 5mL mini-preps for each 15mL culture. *** at the end of all mini-preps, elute the 3 columns pertaining to one plasmid into a single collection epitube ***

steps.title: Measure the plasmid concentration Calculate the...
steps.instruction: Measure the plasmid concentration Calculate the volume required for 8-ug plasmid DNA for each plasmid

steps.title: Prepare plasmids
steps.instruction: Prepare plasmids

steps.title: Pipette 8ug of each plasmid into one 1
steps.instruction: Pipette 8ug of each plasmid into one 1.5mL epitube. Use a speed-vac to concentrate tdNA to a total of 5-10uL

steps.title: Prepare particle bombardment materials - Turn...
steps.instruction: Prepare particle bombardment materials - Turn on helium tank to pressure = 200psi - Obtain vacuum pump and set up with PDS-1000 - Re-suspend tungsten beads in 50% glycerol (vortex to mix) - Obtain a plat-form vortex - Prepare 2.5M CaCl 2 - Prepare 0.1M Spermidine - Prepare 70% EtOH - Obtain 100% EtOH - Obtain Isopropanol - 1 rupture disk - 7 macrocarrier disks - Place Phaeodactylum plate in PCR with the PDS-1000 system to allow the plate to dry before bombardment

steps.title: Mix tungsten bead by vortex Pipette 50uL beads into 1
steps.instruction: Mix tungsten bead by vortex Pipette 50uL beads into 1.5 epitube. Platform vortex for 5 minutes During the 5 minutes, sterilize the 7 macrocarrier disks. - pour 70% EtOH into a petri dish - wash each disk using tweezers by shaking the disk in the 70% EtOH - dry on other half of petri dish

steps.title: After 5 min on plate vortex
steps.instruction: After 5 min on plate vortex... Pipette DNA into tungsten beach Pipette 50uL CaCl2 (2.5M), vortex Pipette 20uL Spermidine (0.1M) to hybridize DNA to beads Mix by flicking tube with finger MANY TIMES. Plate vortex for 3 minutes

steps.title: During 3 minutes
steps.instruction: During 3 minutes.... Place 7 dried macrocarrier disks into the carrier disk manifold. Ensure disks are secured under the lip of each macrocarrier position.

steps.title: After 3 minutes
steps.instruction: After 3 minutes.... Pellet tungsten beads in a table-top centrifuge (fixed rotor) Centrifuge at 8000 rpm for 10 seconds (beads will pellet very easily) Remove supernatent Resuspend in 140uL 100% EtOH, MIX THOROUGHLY by pipette Repeat pelleting and resuspension 2X On last resuspension, add only 100uL 100% EtOH and MIX THOROUGHLY MAINTAIN HOMOGENY BY FLICKING TUBE

steps.title: Pipette 10uL of beads onto each...
steps.instruction: Pipette 10uL of beads onto each macrocarrier disk Allow beads to dry completely on disks

steps.title: While drying
steps.instruction: While drying.... Briefly wash rupture disk in isopropanol Insert "wet" rupture disk into rupture manifold and screw manifold into the bombardment system Secure the metal mesh screen between the two plates of the carrier disk manifold. Insert manifold into square metal fitting and insert into the appropriate slot of the bombardment system. (The rupture manifold should be suspended directly above the carrier disk manifold and aligned with the 7 holes)

steps.title: Place the Phaeodactylum plate below and...
steps.instruction: Place the Phaeodactylum plate below and in line with the carrier disk manifold

steps.title: Shut door to the bombardment system...
steps.instruction: Shut door to the bombardment system Turn on the vacuum manifold. Allow the vacuum pressure to build to 25 inch Hg and hold. Press and hold the "FIRE" button. The pressure will build until ~1300 psi when the rupture disk ruptures. There will be a loud bang! Once the loud bang occurs, "VENT" the bombardment system to remove the vacuum pressure.

steps.title: Place the lid on the Phaeodactylum...
steps.instruction: Place the lid on the Phaeodactylum plate Wrap the plate in tin foil Incubate the plate at 18 o C for 48hrs

steps.title: After 48hrs in the dark
steps.instruction: After 48hrs in the dark... Scrape cells off plate and resuspend in a 2mL epitube with ASW.

steps.title: Prepare selection plates Each plate
steps.instruction: Prepare selection plates Each plate: 15mL 1.6% agarose + 15mL ASW (w/ appropriate N-substrate) + Phleomycin antibiotic (20mg/mL) Dry plates under PCR hood before plating cells

steps.title: For each transformation, dry 3 selection plates
steps.instruction: For each transformation, dry 3 selection plates. Plate all cells on the 3 plates and spread (maximum 400uL per plate) Allow cells to completely dry under PCR hood. *** Phleomycin resistant colonies appear after 3-4 weeks***
